name: Monte Urgull and city tour through the old town of San Sebastian
name_original: Monte Urgull y city tour por el casco antiguo de San Sebastian
region_intro: Jogging routes around Saint-Sébastien offer a diverse range of terrain, from flat coastal paths to challenging mountain trails. The city is situated between the Bay of Biscay and green mountains, providing a varied landscape for runners. Joggers can explore scenic promenades, riverside paths, and hills that offer panoramic city views. This combination of coastal and elevated routes caters to different preferences and fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Saint-Sébastien The most popular jogging route is Playa de La Concha y Peine del Viento, a 3.2 miles (5.1 km) trail that takes about 30 minutes to complete. This route offers a scenic run along the iconic La Concha promenade and beach. Another top favourite among local runners is Monte Urgull and city tour through the old town of San Sebastian, an easy 2.9 miles (4.6 km) path. This route combines a city tour with an ascent of Monte Urgull, providing views of the city and historical sites. Local runners also love the La Concha Beach – San Sebastián City Hall loop from Gros, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through coastal areas and the city center, often completed in about 1 hour 8 minutes. Jogging around Saint-Sébastien is defined by coastal promenades, riverside paths, and hills like Monte Urgull and Monte Ulia. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult running, suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Saint-Sébastien are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 14,000 runners have used komoot to explore Saint-Sébastien's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Saint-Sébastien offers a wide variety of running routes, with over 140 options available. These include 17 easy routes, 97 moderate routes, and 28 challenging trails, catering to all fitness levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Runners in Saint-Sébastien can enjoy a diverse range of terrain. You'll find flat, scenic paths along the iconic La Concha Promenade and the Urumea River, as well as more challenging mountainous trails on peaks like Monte Urgull, Monte Igueldo, and Mount Ulia. This mix provides options from paved surfaces to hilly, natural trails.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly running routes in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Yes, Saint-Sébastien has 17 easy running routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1059016967/monte-urgull-and-city-tour-through-the-old-town-of-san-sebastian" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Urgull and city tour through the old town of San Sebastian&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 2.9-mile (4.6 km) path combining city sights with a gentle ascent.

Q: Can I find longer running routes, for example, around 10k, in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Absolutely. For those looking for longer distances, there are several routes around 10k. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10527136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Concha Beach – San Sebastián City Hall loop from Gros&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.8 miles (10.9 km) and takes about 1 hour 8 minutes to complete, offering coastal views and city exploration.

Q: What are some scenic landmarks or attractions I can see while running in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Many running routes in Saint-Sébastien pass by beautiful landmarks. You can run along &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/567533" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Concha Beach&lt;/a&gt;, past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5577591" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Sebastián City Hall&lt;/a&gt;, or over the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/567551" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kursaal Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, routes ascending &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1041337" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Urgull&lt;/a&gt; offer stunning vistas and historical sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5976191" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sacred Heart Statue&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, Saint-Sébastien offers several circular running routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10527136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Concha Beach – San Sebastián City Hall loop from Gros&lt;/a&gt; is a great circular option that takes you through coastal areas and the city center.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Saint-Sébastien?
A: The running routes in Saint-Sébastien are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from the iconic coastal promenades to the challenging mountain trails, and the breathtaking views offered throughout the region.

Q: Is Saint-Sébastien suitable for trail running with significant elevation gain?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, Saint-Sébastien and the surrounding Basque Country mountains offer routes with significant elevation gains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058554652/monte-ulia-y-cala-irurdieta-circular-trailrunning-por-los-miradores-del-cantabrico" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Ulia y Cala Irurdieta — circular trailrunning por los miradores del Cantábrico&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, features over 340 meters of ascent over 7.5 km, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Saint-Sébastien generally experiences a mild climate throughout the year, making it comfortable for outdoor training regardless of the season. This means you can enjoy running along the coast or in the mountains almost any time.

Q: Are there any running routes that are particularly family-friendly?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly detailed, the numerous easy and moderate routes, especially those along the flat coastal promenade like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1058571451/playa-de-la-concha-y-peine-del-viento" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de La Concha y Peine del Viento&lt;/a&gt;, are generally suitable for families. These offer accessible paths and scenic views without significant challenges.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes in Saint-Sébastien?
A: Many outdoor areas in Saint-Sébastien are dog-friendly, particularly the natural trails and less crowded paths. However, it's always advisable to check local regulations for specific routes, especially in protected areas or on busy promenades, to ensure dogs are permitted and to keep them on a leash where required.
tips: This building is a special highlight in the center of the otherwise architecturally very attractive capital of the province of Gipuzkoa. It's not for nothing that the town hall of San Sebastian is one of the most photographed motifs in the city. The magnificent building impresses with its front made of gold-colored stone, which gives the town hall a noble and radiant appearance.
